_instructions: Fill in fields below. Repeatable sections (parents, grandparents, siblings, children, pets) are arrays — add as many entries as needed. Leave empty strings for unknown fields. Load via: lv80PreloadNarrator(template) or lv80PreloadFromFile(file)

parents.notableLifeEvents: Inspired her children's musical talents through storytelling and singing. Created the 'Coat of Many Colors'.
parents.notes: She had problems during Randy's birth which meant she wasn't home for one Christmas.

parents.notableLifeEvents: Unable to read or write, which inspired the Dolly Parton Imagination Library.

grandparents.memorableStories: Maternal grandfather who was a Pentecostal preacher.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Wrote books about the Parton family such as 'Smoky Mountain Memories'.
siblings.memories: Appeared with siblings on the TV series 'Dolly' in 1977.

siblings.notes: Born in 1942; passed away in November 2024.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Chose a private life out of the spotlight.
siblings.notes: Born in 1948. Father of Danielle Parton who operates a moonshine distillery.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Performed 'Break My Mind' with sisters Cassie and Dolly.
Featured in the Dollywood show 'My People'.
siblings.memories: Guest on 'The Porter Wagoner Show' in 1970.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Had his own show at Dollywood.
siblings.memories: Born in the hospital, unlike some older siblings.

spouse.narrative: Met outside the Wishy Washy Laundromat in Nashville when Dolly was 18 and he was 21. He is described as a deep person with a great and warped sense of humor who prefers to stay in the background.

marriage.weddingDetails: Married May 30, 1966, in Ringgold, GA. A small service attended only by Dolly's mother, the preacher, and his wife.

familyTraditions.description: Family gatherings for Christmas with music, fun, and hay rides.

earlyMemories.firstMemory: The sounds of her environment and her mother's singing.
earlyMemories.favoriteToy: Desired a walking and talking doll like her cousin's, but her father called a new baby brother a 'real doll' instead.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Receiving a guitar from her uncle at age eight.

education.schooling: Attended local schools in Sevier County.
education.higherEducation: First in her family to graduate high school (1964).
education.earlyCareer: Sang on local radio stations at age ten and performed at the Grand Ole Opry at age thirteen.
education.careerProgression: Moved to Nashville the day after high school graduation. Between 1964 and 1980, produced many hits and won her first Grammy. Movie debut in '9 to 5' (1980).
education.communityInvolvement: Founded the Dollywood Foundation and Imagination Library (1995) to foster a love of reading.

laterYears.retirement: Has stated she does not plan to retire, preferring to stay active and inspired.
laterYears.lifeLessons: 'Don't get so busy making a living that you forget to make a life'. 'Find out who you are. Then do it on purpose'.
laterYears.advice for Future Generations: 'Dream More, Learn More, Care More and Be More'.

hobbies.hobbies: Reading, cooking, and traveling in an RV.
hobbies.personalChallenges: Growing up in extreme poverty in the Smoky Mountains.
hobbies.travel: Enjoys traveling simply with her husband in their RV.

health.wellnessTips: 'If you want the rainbow, you have to put up with the rain'.

additionalNotes.unfinishedDreams: Continuing to expand the Imagination Library to children worldwide.
additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: 'If you don't like the road you're walking, start paving another one'.
